assess.title: Run Unit 3 End-of-Unit Assessment
assess.desc: Administer IM Unit 3 assessment covering linear relationships, slope, y = mx + b, and calculating slope from two points

revise.title: Revise Unit 4 Lessons 1–4 (Coteach)
revise.desc: Use Coteach to adjust Unit 4 lessons on equivalent equations and balanced moves — add scaffolding for students who struggled with slope and y-intercept in Unit 3

oko-slope.title: OKO Small Group: Calculating Slope
oko-slope.desc: Jayden, Aisha, and Carlos all struggled with calculating slope from two points on the Unit 3 assessment. Set them up with OKO to practice finding slope and interpreting rate of change in real-world contexts from Lessons 7 and 10.

marcus.title: Extra Practice: Marcus
marcus.desc: Marcus confuses slope and y-intercept, so he needs support from Unit 3 Lessons 5–8. Assign him 1-on-1 practice on writing equations in y = mx + b form from a graph.

aaliyah.title: Extra Practice: Aaliyah
aaliyah.desc: Aaliyah has a gap from Grade 7 Unit 2 on proportional relationships (y = kx) that’s blocking her understanding of linear vs. proportional. Assign her 1-on-1 practice identifying the constant of proportionality.
aaliyah.math: y = kx: if 3 pizzas cost $24, find k

oko-equations.title: OKO Group: Writing Equations from Tables
oko-equations.desc: Maya, DeShawn, and Imani can read tables but aren’t connecting rate of change to slope yet. Set them up with OKO to practice writing y = mx + b from tables, building on Unit 3 Lessons 5–7.

challenge.title: Challenge: Equation Error Analysis
challenge.desc: Darius and Zara aced the Unit 3 assessment and are ready to move ahead. Give them Unit 4 Lesson 4 cool-down style problems where they critique solution paths and find errors in multi-step equations.

analyze.title: Analyze Practice for Common Bugs
analyze.desc: Review Unit 3 cool-downs and practice problems to find patterns in student errors around slope, y-intercept, and writing equations of lines
analyze.math: Bug: reads y-int as slope in y = 3 + 2x

video.title: Create Video: Slope from Two Points
video.desc: Record explainer video students watch at home on calculating slope from two points, using rise/run and the formula — the most common Unit 3 gap

game.title: Build Hanger Diagram Game
game.desc: Create an interactive game where students balance hanger diagrams and match them to equations — supports Unit 4 Lessons 2–3 conceptual foundation

assign-game.title: Assign Game to Class
assign-game.desc: Push the hanger diagram game to all students as practice before Unit 4 Lesson 3 Balanced Moves

timeline.text: ── Monday, Nov 3 ──
Okay, starting Unit 4 today! We just finished Unit 3 and I’m worried about some of my kids.
I’ve set up your board for the transition into Unit 4: Linear Equations. Here are the tasks I’d recommend based on what I know about your class. Please review and approve each one.
✓ 11 tasks created · 4 with dependencies
This looks good. Let me go through them.
Hold off on the extension for Darius and Zara. I want to see how they do on the assessment first before sending them ahead.
Got it — I’ll keep the challenge card on hold until we have assessment results.
✓ 10 approved · 1 on hold

Starting the Unit 3 assessment and the OKO equations group right away. Key areas to watch on the assessment: slope from two points (Lesson 10), writing y = mx + b (Lesson 8), and interpreting slope in context (Lessons 6–7).
→ Assessment & OKO Equations → In Progress
OKO session in progress: Maya, DeShawn, and Imani are working on writing y = mx + b from tables. OKO is having them identify the rate of change between rows before writing the equation.
OKO session complete! Maya nailed rate of change from tables (92%). DeShawn (85%) still sometimes confuses the initial value with the first y-value. Imani (88%) is solid.
✓ “OKO Group: Writing Equations from Tables” → Done
── Tuesday, Nov 4 ──
Assessment results are in. Here’s what I found:

• 58% struggled to calculate slope from two coordinate points (Lesson 10 skill)
• 6 students can’t reliably write y = mx + b from a graph
• Aaliyah, Jayden, and 3 others still shaky on proportional relationships (Grade 7 gap)
• Darius and Zara aced everything — ready for extension

This matters because Unit 4 Lessons 1–4 assume students can recognize equivalent forms of equations.
→ “Run Unit 3 Assessment” → Review
Okay, those results confirm what I was seeing in class. Go ahead and start Marcus and the slope group. And since Darius and Zara clearly don’t need the review, let’s approve that challenge card now.

Approved! Starting targeted interventions. OKO will run the slope group with Jayden, Aisha, and Carlos using real-world rate-of-change contexts from Lessons 6–7. Marcus gets Brisk practice on slope vs. y-intercept.
→ OKO Slope & Marcus → In Progress

Marcus had a breakthrough on Brisk! He was reading the coefficient of x as the y-intercept. After working through three graphs where he plotted the y-intercept first, then used slope to find a second point, he got 11/12 correct.
That’s great about Marcus. I noticed the same thing in class — he kept mixing up m and b. Glad Brisk caught it.
✓ “Extra Practice: Marcus” → Done
── Wednesday, Nov 5 ──

OKO slope group wrapped up. Aisha and Carlos can now calculate slope from two points. Jayden still reverses rise and run sometimes — flagging for follow-up. Starting Aaliyah’s Brisk practice on proportional relationships.
✓ OKO Slope group → Done
Assessment analysis approved. Unlocking the lesson revision task. I’ll use coteach.ai to adjust Unit 4 Lessons 1–4 based on what we learned.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Assessment → Lesson Revision

coteach.ai is adjusting the lessons. For Lesson 1 (Writing Equivalent Equations), adding a warm-up reviewing what “equivalent” means with simple number examples. For Lesson 2 (Keeping the Equation Balanced), adding extra hanger diagram practice.
Aaliyah completed her Brisk practice. She can now distinguish y = kx from y = mx + b. Darius and Zara are starting the error analysis challenge.
✓ Aaliyah → Done · Challenge group started
── Thursday, Nov 6 ──
coteach.ai finished the lesson revisions. Starting practice analysis and the hanger diagram game today.
→ Lesson revision → Review · Analysis & Game → In Progress

Practice analysis complete. Top 3 bugs across the class:
1. Confusing slope and y-intercept when equation is written as y = b + mx (41%)
2. Calculating slope as run/rise instead of rise/run (28%)
3. Not recognizing that a negative slope means the line goes down (19%)

Creating a targeted video on slope from two points.
That y = b + mx confusion is real — I see it every year. Make sure the video shows both forms side by side.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Analysis → Video creation
── Friday, Nov 7 ──

Last day! Video is rendered with both equation forms shown side by side as you requested. Hanger diagram game is ready for review. Darius and Zara crushed the error analysis — they found all 4 errors in Lin’s solution path.
→ Video & Game → Review · Challenge → Done
The revised lessons look great. Go ahead and push the video for weekend homework and assign the hanger game before Monday.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Game → Assign to class

Week complete! 🎉

✅ 11/11 tasks done
📊 Unit 3 assessed — slope from two points was the biggest gap
✏️ Unit 4 Lessons 1–4 revised via coteach.ai
👥 Both OKO groups closed key gaps
👤 Marcus and Aaliyah caught up via Brisk
🌟 Darius & Zara completed error analysis extension
🎬 Slope video assigned for homework
🎮 Hanger diagram game assigned for Lesson 2 prep
Great week. Feeling much better about starting Unit 4 on Monday. 🙏
✓ All tasks complete — ready to launch Unit 4 on Monday!
